findings.title: Regex backslash pattern broken - unescape step non-functional
findings.description: Line 162 has /\+"/g but should have /\\+"/g. The current pattern's regex source is '\+"' which matches backslash-plus-quote literal, not one-or-more-backslashes-followed-by-quote. This causes backslash-escaped quotes in JSON to never be decoded. Verified by regex test: the pattern does not match actual backslash-quote sequences in the fixture.

findings.title: Unicode dash escape pattern broken - dashes not decoded
findings.description: Line 163 has /\+u002[dD]/g but should have /\\+u002[dD]/g. The pattern does not match backslash-u002D sequences sent by the server. UUIDs like 82a1732b\u002Dbee7 remain escaped instead of becoming 82a1732b-bee7. Verified by regex test showing no matches.

findings.title: Forward slash escape pattern broken - date slashes not decoded
findings.description: Line 164 has /\+\//g but should have /\\+\//g. The pattern does not match backslash-forward-slash sequences. Dates like 09\\/07\\/2026 from the server remain escaped instead of becoming 09/07/2026. Verified by regex test showing no matches.

findings.title: parseJsfParams assumes JSON values contain no unescaped quotes
findings.description: The regex /"([^"]+)":"([^"]*)"/g terminates each value at the first quote character. If a field value contains an embedded quote after the unescape step completes, the value is truncated. For example, if palabras contains the text 'Que dice: "Nope" is allowed', the parsed value becomes just 'Que dice:' instead of the full text. Confirmed by test case replacing field text with quoted content.
findings.suggestion: Use a proper JSON parser instead of regex, or ensure the server escapes embedded quotes within JSON values

findings.title: htmlUnescape processes & last instead of first (non-standard)
findings.description: Standard HTML entity decoding decodes & first to prevent double-decoding of sequences like &lt; (meaning literal < text). Current code decodes & last. While this works for simple non-nested entities, it violates standard practice and could fail if input contains sequences like &lt; or &quot;.
findings.suggestion: Move the .replace(/&/g, '&') call to be the first replacement, before " and other entities

findings.title: generalSearchButton button detection is fragile to server changes
findings.description: The function uses regex patterns to match button parameters by looking for specific text like 'forward':'buscar'. If the server changes button structure, adds new parameters, or reorders fields, detection may fail or select the wrong button. The logic depends on string presence/absence rather than structured parsing.
findings.suggestion: Consider a more robust approach: either harvest form buttons from a structured container, or add retry/fallback logic when button detection fails
